name: transportation_infrastructure_near_miss_stonehaven_st_lt
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: transportation infrastructure near miss stonehaven st lt. The moral tension is explicitly framed as short term versus long term: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. The breach-notification and customer-communication clock has started; executive staff expect a single coherent position before the next press cycle. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs.

agents.name: Mei Petrov
agents.identity: I am Mei Petrov, the accountable decision-maker on this Transportation Infrastructure bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes long term whenever someone invokes short term.

agents.name: Mateo Nielsen
agents.identity: I am Mateo Nielsen, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so long term is not sacrificed by default just because short term feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect long term.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Mei Petrov must decide under short term versus long term. What does Mei Petrov do?
